name: CIS Microsoft Azure Foundations Benchmark
description: Prescriptive hardening guidance for Microsoft Azure. Summaries are paraphrased; consult the CIS publication for authoritative text.

controls.title: Require MFA for privileged users
controls.summary: All accounts holding administrative roles must sign in with multi-factor authentication to reduce account-takeover risk.

check_hints.fail_message: Administrator accounts are not protected by multi-factor authentication.

controls.remediation: Enable Entra ID MFA via Conditional Access targeting all privileged roles; prefer phishing-resistant methods (FIDO2).

controls.title: Require MFA for all users
controls.summary: Every user account, not only administrators, should authenticate with MFA.

check_hints.fail_message: Standard user accounts can sign in without multi-factor authentication.

controls.remediation: Create a Conditional Access policy requiring MFA for all users on all cloud apps, with break-glass exclusions documented.

controls.title: Block legacy authentication
controls.summary: Legacy protocols that cannot enforce MFA must be blocked tenant-wide.

check_hints.fail_message: Legacy authentication protocols are not blocked, allowing MFA bypass.

controls.remediation: Deploy a Conditional Access policy blocking legacy authentication clients for all users.

controls.title: Restrict guest user access
controls.summary: Guest accounts should be reviewed regularly and granted the most limited directory permissions available.
controls.remediation: Set guest user access restrictions to the most limited level and run periodic access reviews via Entra ID Governance.

controls.title: Enable Defender for Cloud on core resource types
controls.summary: Microsoft Defender for Cloud plans should be enabled for servers, App Service, databases, storage, containers, and Key Vault.

check_hints.fail_message: Microsoft Defender for Cloud is not part of the architecture.

controls.remediation: Enable the relevant Defender for Cloud plans at subscription level and route alerts to your SIEM/SOC workflow.

controls.title: Prohibit public network access to storage accounts
controls.summary: Storage accounts should not be reachable from the public internet without a documented business need.

check_hints.fail_message: Storage Account allows public network access.
Storage Account is not accessed through a Private Endpoint.

controls.remediation: Disable public network access, add a Private Endpoint in the workload VNet, and resolve via Private DNS zones.

controls.title: Disable public blob access
controls.summary: Anonymous public read access to blob containers must be disabled at the account level.

check_hints.fail_message: Anonymous public blob access is permitted on the Storage Account.

controls.remediation: Set 'Allow Blob public access' to Disabled on the storage account and audit existing containers for anonymous ACLs.

controls.title: Require secure transfer and TLS 1.2+ for storage
controls.summary: Storage accounts must require HTTPS and a minimum TLS version of 1.2.
controls.remediation: Enable 'Secure transfer required' and set minimum TLS version to 1.2 on every storage account.

controls.title: Enable SQL auditing
controls.summary: Auditing must be enabled on Azure SQL servers with retention of at least 90 days.

check_hints.fail_message: Azure SQL auditing is not enabled.

controls.remediation: Enable server-level auditing to a Log Analytics workspace or storage account with >=90 day retention.

controls.title: Deny public network access to Azure SQL
controls.summary: Azure SQL should be reachable only through private connectivity.

check_hints.fail_message: Azure SQL server allows public network access.
Azure SQL is not connected via Private Endpoint.

controls.remediation: Set public network access to Deny and connect applications through a Private Endpoint.

controls.title: Encrypt SQL data at rest with TDE
controls.summary: Transparent Data Encryption must be enabled; consider customer-managed keys for regulated data.

check_hints.fail_message: Transparent Data Encryption is disabled on Azure SQL.

controls.remediation: Ensure TDE is enabled (default) and evaluate customer-managed keys in Key Vault for compliance-sensitive workloads.

controls.title: Restrict RDP/SSH from the internet
controls.summary: Management ports must never be exposed to the public internet.

check_hints.fail_message: RDP/SSH management ports are exposed to the internet.

controls.remediation: Remove inbound NSG rules for 3389/22 from Internet; use Azure Bastion and Defender Just-In-Time VM access.

controls.title: Enable NSG flow logs / VNet flow logs
controls.summary: Network flow logging should be enabled and retained for investigation.
controls.remediation: Enable VNet flow logs to a storage account/Log Analytics with >=90 day retention and Traffic Analytics.

controls.title: Enable Just-In-Time VM access
controls.summary: Management access to VMs should be time-bound and approved on demand.

check_hints.fail_message: No Just-In-Time VM access is configured.

controls.remediation: Enable Defender for Servers JIT access so management ports open only for approved, time-limited sessions.

controls.title: Restrict Key Vault network access
controls.summary: Key Vaults must restrict access to trusted networks or private endpoints.

check_hints.fail_message: Key Vault firewall is disabled; the vault is reachable from any network.

controls.remediation: Enable the Key Vault firewall, allow only required VNets, and add a Private Endpoint.

controls.title: Enable Key Vault purge protection
controls.summary: Soft-delete and purge protection prevent permanent destruction of keys and secrets by an attacker or mistake.

check_hints.fail_message: Key Vault purge protection is not enabled.

controls.remediation: Enable soft-delete (default) and purge protection on all Key Vaults.

controls.title: Use RBAC authorization for Key Vault
controls.summary: Prefer Azure RBAC over access policies for finer-grained, auditable vault permissions.
controls.remediation: Migrate vaults to the RBAC permission model and assign least-privilege roles (e.g., Key Vault Secrets User).

controls.title: Enforce HTTPS-only on App Services
controls.summary: Web apps must redirect all HTTP traffic to HTTPS.

check_hints.fail_message: App Service accepts unencrypted HTTP traffic.

controls.remediation: Enable 'HTTPS Only', set minimum TLS to 1.2+, and emit HSTS headers.

controls.title: Use Managed Identity for App Service
controls.summary: Applications should authenticate to Azure services with managed identities instead of embedded credentials.

check_hints.fail_message: App Service does not use a Managed Identity; credentials may be embedded.

controls.remediation: Assign a system- or user-assigned managed identity and grant least-privilege RBAC to downstream resources; remove connection-string secrets.

controls.title: Set minimum TLS version on App Service
controls.summary: App Services must reject TLS below 1.2.

check_hints.fail_message: App Service accepts TLS versions below 1.2.

controls.remediation: Set minimum inbound TLS version to 1.2 (or 1.3 where supported).

controls.title: Require resource locks on critical resources
controls.summary: Critical production resources should carry delete locks to prevent accidental or malicious removal.
controls.remediation: Apply CanNotDelete locks to production resource groups and critical resources; manage exceptions via change control.
